flanqr__26: Do standardized tests improve education  Standardized test score inflation
ensemble__26: Do standardized tests improve education  Standardized tests are sometimes used as a way to measure whether educators are making progress or not  especially in states that have adopted high stakes tests  Do standardized tests improve education  Standardized testing is commonly done in grades 3 6  although the levels of standards in schools are not the only element in student achievement  other elements such as teacher parent relations and peer pressure are also important  Standardized test scores do not correlate with improvements in student achievement  but may be overestimated  parents and students assume high scores indicate improvement  but research has shown them to be poor predictors of improved student performance  and students  scoring on standardized tests is strongly associated with their grade placement  Standardized tests can tell us which students are doing well and where we can adjust instruction to get the most out of our teachers  but they can also be very counterproductive to a teacher in school  Standardized tests can be used to evaluate a school and to improve that school   but these tests are hardly an independent authority   standardized tests do not always accurately reflect real life   and test score inflation does not increase the value of a high school diploma for most students   Standardized tests are most beneficial for improving public school standards   helping students to succeed in reading and math classes   and validating teachers and administrators   education  standardized test If these tests reveal that an student is struggling  this knowledge can be used to direct educational interventions in a way that is most beneficial to students  However  some education experts say that standardized tests are actually a drain on teachers  time and can negatively impact learning  In an effort to improve the public school system in the United States  states have attempted to institute standardized tests to make it easier for parents to select schools for their children  to make graduation requirements clear  and to compare the performance of students from different schools
